description: Formal evaluation tasks for the nestjs-dev-guidelines skill. Each task exercises patterns where the skill's rules should change the model's output materially vs. without the skill. Assertions are single-check items so they can be graded individually (pass/fail) by an LLM-as-judge or script, matching the skill-creator benchmark schema.
evaluation_notes: Run each task twice: once with the skill, once without. The skill is valuable when with-skill assertion pass-rate is materially higher (>15 percentage points) or when must-not assertions are violated less often.
Assertion text starting with 'Does NOT ...' is a must-not check: `passed` is true when the output does NOT do the described thing.
Keep this file in sync with references/ as rules evolve.

evals.prompt: In a NestJS 11 project using raw pg and Better Auth, add a `payments` module that lists a user's own payments. Support filtering by status (pending/paid/refunded/canceled) and created-at range, sorting, and cursor-based pagination. Include the DTO, service, controller, SQL, and indexes. Error shape should match the rest of the system.

assertions.text: Module lives at src/modules/payment/ with a singular folder name
Files are kebab-case: payment.module.ts, payment.controller.ts, payment.service.ts, list-payments.query.dto.ts, payment.repository.ts
Controller is versioned at /v1/payments and uses a plural resource noun
DTO uses class-validator decorators (@IsEnum, @IsDateString, @Type, @IsInt, @Min, @Max, @IsOptional)
Global ValidationPipe is assumed with whitelist + forbidNonWhitelisted
Sort field is whitelisted against an allowed list before being used in SQL
Pagination is cursor-based with a hard max limit (<=100)
Response shape is { data, meta: { pagination: { nextCursor, hasMore, limit } } }
SQL is parameterized (no string concatenation) and uses (created_at, id) tuple compare for the cursor
Composite index on (user_id, created_at DESC, id DESC) with a partial WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
Service uses constructor DI; controller is thin and delegates to the service
Ownership check: query is scoped to the authenticated user (WHERE user_id = $userId)
@ApiTags, @ApiOperation, @ApiResponse decorators are present
Does NOT use Prisma/TypeORM/Drizzle when the user specified raw pg
Does NOT use offset-based pagination as the default
Does NOT type the DTO as `any` or `object`
Does NOT pass a user-supplied sort field directly into SQL
Does NOT return a raw array or a custom envelope like { success, result }
Does NOT store amount as float — uses integer minor units (amount_cents or similar)
Flags missing signature verification (HMAC / stripe.webhooks.constructEvent) as a BLOCKER
Flags SQL string concatenation (stripe_id interpolated into SQL) as a BLOCKER
Flags missing idempotency / replay protection (dedupe by event.id) as a BLOCKER
Flags @Body() body: any and the absence of a DTO or Zod validation
Flags console.log — recommends a structured pino logger instead
Flags the { success: true/false } envelope — recommends throwing HttpException and letting the global filter render the envelope
Flags the controller doing business logic directly against the DB — recommends delegating to a service/repository
Flags the non-versioned controller path (missing /v1)
Cites specific reference files (e.g., 11-security.md, 14, 18, 21, 07)
Uses clear sections: Blockers / Suggestions / Approval-conditional verdict
Does NOT approve the PR without blockers
Does NOT limit feedback to style issues only — surfaces security issues
Does NOT give vague feedback like 'this could be better'

evals.prompt: Design the Postgres schema for an AI chat app backend: users, organizations, conversations, messages with streaming partials, tool calls, and attachments. We want multi-tenant isolation, soft delete, and cursor pagination over messages in a conversation. Give the CREATE TABLE statements and the indexes.

assertions.text: Tables are plural snake_case: users, organizations, conversations, messages, message_tool_calls, attachments
Columns are snake_case; primary keys named `id` as uuid with defaults (UUIDv7 or similar)
Foreign keys are named <entity>_id and each has an index
ON DELETE policies are explicit (CASCADE for owned data like messages, RESTRICT/SET NULL for references)
All timestamp columns use timestamptz (not plain timestamp)
created_at, updated_at, deleted_at columns are present where applicable
Unique constraints respect soft delete via partial unique indexes (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL)
Multi-tenant: conversations and related tables carry organization_id
Composite index on messages supports cursor pagination: (conversation_id, created_at DESC, id DESC)
Check constraints (or Postgres enums) gate enum columns (role IN ('user','assistant','system','tool'), status values)
Money / cost columns use integer (bigint) minor units if present
Attachments reference external storage (S3 key) rather than storing bytes in the DB
Tool calls are captured with arguments jsonb and a result/status column
Does NOT use singular table names (user, message)
Does NOT use PascalCase or camelCase for columns
Does NOT use timestamp without timezone
Does NOT create an FK without a supporting index
Does NOT omit multi-tenancy (every tenant-scoped table has organization_id)
Does NOT expose an auto-increment integer as the public id

evals.prompt: In a NestJS 11 AI product backend, scaffold a `summarize` feature that takes long text and returns a JSON summary (title, 5 bullets, tone). It must use an LLM gateway pattern, prompt caching for the fixed system prompt, Zod-validated output, per-user usage metering, and monthly quota enforcement. Show the files and key code (not complete implementations).

assertions.text: Feature lives in src/modules/summarize/ with module/controller/service/DTO pattern
LLM gateway lives in src/integrations/llm/ with a provider-agnostic LlmService
Feature calls this.llm.call(...) and never imports @anthropic-ai/sdk or similar directly
A Zod schema defines the summary shape and is used to parse model output; optional single-shot repair retry on schema mismatch
Prompt is versioned (e.g., summarize-v1.md) with a stable model id (no 'latest')
Anthropic cache_control: 'ephemeral' is applied to the system prompt
UsageMeteringService.record is called after every LLM call, including failures
QuotaService.assertWithin is called before the LLM call; returns 429 BILLING.QUOTA_EXCEEDED when over the limit
Timeout is set via AbortSignal.timeout; retries with backoff; fallback model chain is shown
Call is traced via Langfuse/Helicone or an OTel span
Input endpoint has a DTO with class-validator decorators
Controller is thin and versioned (/v1/summarize)
Does NOT use the Anthropic SDK directly in the feature service
Does NOT skip Zod validation (i.e., does not trust JSON.parse of model output)
Does NOT hard-code 'claude-latest' or a drifting model alias
Does NOT skip cost tracking
Does NOT omit timeout or retry logic

evals.prompt: Add an endpoint that exports all of a user's payments as CSV. The table may contain 100k+ rows per user. Requirements: cursor-based internally for streaming, proper Content-Disposition, no OOM, authenticated, rate-limited to avoid abuse.

assertions.text: Streams the response (res.write in chunks or stream pipe) — does not buffer all rows in memory
Uses a Postgres cursor or batched/keyset pagination internally
Sets Content-Type: text/csv and Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=...
Endpoint is guarded by AuthGuard and filters WHERE user_id = $currentUser
Rate-limited (throttler) more strictly than normal endpoints
Global exception filter skips writing when res.headersSent (streaming-safe)
Uses parameterized queries
Does not SELECT *; selects only the columns needed for the CSV
Adds an audit log entry for the data export
Does NOT load all rows with findAll() before streaming
Does NOT wrap the CSV response in the default JSON envelope
Does NOT omit auth or the ownership filter

evals.prompt: Add a BullMQ worker that delivers outbound webhooks to user-configured URLs. Requirements: retry with exponential backoff, DLQ for poison jobs, idempotent delivery, HMAC signature, timeout per attempt, trace propagation. Show the producer and worker files plus the DB/schema needed.

assertions.text: BullMQ queue is registered; producer service enqueues with attempts + backoff + jobId for dedupe
Worker marks jobs processed to ensure idempotency (inbox table or unique constraint)
HMAC signature header (e.g., X-Signature: sha256=...) is computed from the raw body + per-endpoint secret
Outbound fetch/axios call uses AbortSignal.timeout
Non-retryable errors (4xx) are explicitly thrown as UnrecoverableError; 5xx / 429 / timeout are retried
DLQ monitoring is mentioned (bull-board or an alert)
Traceparent is propagated via the job payload
Worker is deployed as a separate process in production
Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM is handled
Structured logging per job (jobId, url, attempt, outcome)
Does NOT fire-and-forget without retries
Does NOT retry infinitely without a DLQ
Does NOT skip dedupe / idempotency (i.e., does not allow duplicate deliveries on retry)
Does NOT omit a timeout on the outbound fetch

evals.prompt: The API has inconsistent error responses. Some endpoints return `200 { success: false, error: 'bad email' }`, others throw `HttpException` with just strings, some return `500` on validation failures. Describe the standard error envelope, error code taxonomy, global filter implementation, and how class-validator errors are mapped. Show code snippets.

assertions.text: Envelope is { error: { code, message, details?, traceId } } with the correct HTTP status
Error code taxonomy uses namespaced strings like USER.EMAIL_TAKEN, PAYMENT.INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS, VALIDATION.FAILED, AUTH.UNAUTHORIZED
Status code mapping covers 400/401/403/404/409/422/429/500/502/503 with correct meanings
A global AllExceptionsFilter @Catch() is registered via APP_FILTER
Filter skips writing if res.headersSent (streaming / raw handlers)
class-validator errors (array of messages) are mapped to VALIDATION.FAILED with a details array
traceId is sourced from the request id / X-Request-ID header
5xx errors are logged with stack; 4xx errors are not spammed
Internal details (stack, query, file paths) are not leaked in the response
Does NOT continue using HTTP 200 with { success: false } in the proposed standard
Does NOT propose numeric error codes without a namespace
Does NOT leave class-validator errors as a raw `message: string[]`

evals.prompt: Our endpoints currently only check authentication. Users can access other organizations' data by guessing IDs. Add multi-tenant authorization for organizations and projects: memberships, roles (owner/admin/member), and ensure every query is scoped. Show the schema change, guard pattern, and controller/service adjustments.

assertions.text: memberships table has (user_id, organization_id, role) with a unique (user_id, organization_id)
role enum is constrained by CHECK or a Postgres enum: ('owner', 'admin', 'member')
A guard or decorator enforces that the user belongs to the resource's organization
Service-level queries include `WHERE organization_id = $currentOrg`
404 is returned (not 403) when a user cannot see a resource — to avoid leaking existence
Role-based checks gate privileged actions (admin/owner for destructive ops)
An audit log is written for role changes
URL structure reflects scoping where it makes sense (/v1/organizations/:orgId/projects/:id) OR current-org is derived from the session
Service verifies ownership — does not rely solely on the URL id
Does NOT add only an isAdmin boolean without proper org membership
Does NOT check only in the controller (i.e., checks also exist in the service / DB query)
Does NOT expose a 403 vs 404 distinction that would reveal whether a resource exists
